title: Shared drop directory: one job's files keep getting deleted by another
description_md:
`/srv/shared/dropbox` is a shared scratch directory where a few unrelated batch-job accounts (`batchjob`, `otherjob`) hand files off to each other, the same way `/tmp` works system-wide. Files owned by one job keep disappearing before they're picked up -- another job's own cleanup is sweeping the whole directory, not just its own files. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), work out what protection this kind of shared directory is missing, and add it.

You're done when a file owned by `batchjob` can no longer be deleted by `otherjob`.
